In honor of his special day, we thought we'd celebrate some of the victories Democrats have delivered since he took office. Under President Biden, we've made once-in-a-lifetime investments in working-class communities like ours:
* We passed the _**CHIPS and Science Act,**_ a historic bill to revitalize American manufacturing, bring good-paying jobs back home, and train our workforce to lead the industries of the future.
* We're rebuilding our communities through the _**Bipartisan Infrastructure Law**_ , a massive, community-by-community investment in our roads, bridges, railways, drinking water systems, and more.
* We made the biggest clean-energy investment in history when we passed the _**Inflation Reduction Act**_ , which is already bringing good-paying green jobs to our communities and lowering energy costs for millions of families.

In all of these bills and more, Marcy has fought to bring federal money and good-paying jobs back to our Heartland — and she's not stopping any time soon. | | Derek, I’m passing along an urgent request from my colleagues Jon Tester and Sherrod Brown: they’re teaming up to raise $500,000 in 48 hours and they need our help. __[**Chip in $5 by midnight tomorrow to support Jon Tester and Sherrod Brown.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/73698692/439374350/1172185174?refcode=mw_em_231120_1&amount=5&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&addr1=&city=Manassas&state=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzNjQ1NzQxZi1lZTg3LWVlMTEtODkyNS0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=1V09sjIAh7cgiO-5BPSAw5xo8LZuoQSNkRNFF1EOg-s=)__ Their states, Montana and Ohio, are going to be two of our absolute toughest Senate battlegrounds next fall. Special interests are expected to spend millions in both states and both campaigns need resources to fight back and keep our Democratic majority. And while Jon and Sherrod say they won’t win the Senate’s best dressed awards, they both deserve awards for their effectiveness as legislators and their commitment to their constituents. We need their voices in the Senate. Thanks, Mark _——Original message from Senators Jon Tester and Sherrod Brown——_ Hi Derek — Jon Tester and Sherrod Brown here. We’re reaching out today because we’re in the middle of the two most competitive Senate campaigns, and we need your help to win. We’re not the typical politicians you’d expect to see every day in the Senate. One of us is a seven-fingered dirt farmer. The other has been called disheveled, wrinkled, and rumpled. Neither of us are winning any best dressed awards anytime soon. But the thing that _really_ sets us apart? We’ll work with anyone to get the job done for our constituents. And we’ll always stand up to the special interests in Washington to help folks back home. But we’re facing the toughest reelection fight of our careers. Election experts are calling us the most vulnerable Democratic senators, and Mitch McConnell and his cronies are planning to unleash hundreds of millions of dollars to try and defeat us. We’ve got a lot of work to do between now and Election Day. **So we’re launching the Big Sky Buckeye Fund with a goal of raising _$500,000_ in the next 48 hours** to build up our resources and fight back against the millions that’ll be thrown our way. __[**Ready to roll up your sleeves and get to work? Become a founding donor of our Big Sky Buckeye Fund by splitting a contribution between our campaigns today. Your support will help us meet our goal, build our resources, and keep fighting for the hardworking people of Montana and Ohio in the name: Jocelyn Benson
subject: Supporting Democracy Defenders in Michigan
disclaimer_text:
Paid for by
Jocelyn Benson for Secretary of State
body:
[https://als-totem.s3.amazonaws.com/core/spacer.gif] Friend, when we launched the Michigan Legacy PAC, we made a commitment to expand the pro-democracy network you helped us build. [https://als-totem.s3.amazonaws.com/core/spacer.gif]
That’s why I’m proud to announce our first endorsements of the 2024 cycle. We’re proudly standing with three Michigan State Representatives – and they’re democracy defenders who are in critical races next fall.
Betsy Coffia, Jenn Hill, and Reggie Miller led the passage to enable pre-registration of 16 and 17-year-olds, fought for government transparency, and supported funding for early voting. They are crucial allies in the necessary work of building democracy, and with a razor-thin majority in the State House, we need every seat.
Today, I’m asking you to join me and pitch in to support Representatives Coffia, Hill, and Miller with a split contribution, as well as continue supporting our work to defend democracy statewide. Will you please pitch in to help us hold a pro-democracy majority in the Michigan House of Representatives? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mlp-endorsements?refcode=112023]
As we head into the Thanksgiving season, I’m thankful for their leadership, and I’m grateful for the work they and other leaders around the state are doing to fortify our democracy.
Our goal is to build on the momentum we saw in 2022 and create a true grassroots network of democracy advocates around the state and nationwide. Your support is critical to that work.
I’ll be in touch soon with more pro-democracy leaders you can help and additional ways to actively uphold these values each day.
Thank you for all that you do for democracy.
Onward,
Jocelyn

clean_body: [] Friend, when we launched the Michigan Legacy PAC, we made a commitment to expand the pro-democracy network you helped us build. [] That’s why I’m proud to announce our first endorsements of the 2024 cycle. We’re proudly standing with three Michigan State Representatives – and they’re democracy defenders who are in critical races next fall. Betsy Coffia, Jenn Hill, and Reggie Miller led the passage to enable pre-registration of 16 and 17-year-olds, fought for government transparency, and supported funding for early voting. They are crucial allies in the necessary work of building democracy, and with a razor-thin majority in the State House, we need every seat. Today, I’m asking you to join me and pitch in to support Representatives Coffia, Hill, and Miller with a split contribution, as well as continue supporting our work to defend democracy statewide. Will you please pitch in to help us hold a pro-democracy majority in the Michigan House of Representatives? [] As we head into the Thanksgiving season, I’m thankful for their leadership, and I’m grateful for the work they and other leaders around the state are doing to fortify our democracy. Our goal is to build on the momentum we saw in 2022 and create a true grassroots network of democracy advocates around the state and nationwide. Your support is critical to that work. I’ll be in touch soon with more pro-democracy leaders you can help and additional ways to actively uphold these values each day. Thank you for all that you do for democracy. Onward, Jocelyn secure.actblue.com/donate/mlp-endorsements?refcode=112023 [secure.actblue.com/donate/mlp-endorsements?refcode=112023] [] This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. name: Elizabeth Warren
subject: Republicans are going all out to defeat these two Senators
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warren for Senate
body:
Mitch McConnell and the GOP are strategizing right now on how to win back control in 2024.

| | | [ ](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/216?t=3&akid=1824%2E4032634%2EO7MTjH)
---
In a moment, [**I’m going to ask if you can make a split donation to support Jon Tester in Montana and Sherrod Brown in Ohio.**](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/984?t=8&akid=1824%2E4032634%2EO7MTjH) But first, I want to take a moment to talk about why it’s so important to support these two races right now. Democrats expanded our Senate majority during the 2022 midterms — that’s due to grassroots supporters chipping in, volunteering, and jumping into the fight. But it’s still just about as slim as can be. Mitch McConnell and the GOP are strategizing right now on how to win back control in 2024, and they’ve drawn big red circles around two critical states: Montana and Ohio. Jon Tester and Sherrod Brown are two of the most vulnerable Democratic senators up for re-election next year. They’re the only Democratic incumbents running in states that Trump won in 2020. So if we want to not only keep but expand our Senate majority even more to make the big, structural change that our country needs, we need to invest in these two critical races as early as we can. [**So can you chip in $28 or anything you can to support Jon Tester in Montana, Sherrod Brown in Ohio, and our re-election campaign?**](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/984?amount=28&t=9&akid=1824%2E4032634%2EO7MTjH)

---
_If you've saved payment info with ActBlue Express, your donation will process automatically:_ |
---
| [DONATE NOW: $3](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/984?t=23&express_lane=true&amount=3&akid=1824%2E4032634%2EO7MTjH)
---
| [DONATE NOW: $15](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/984?t=25&express_lane=true&amount=15&akid=1824%2E4032634%2EO7MTjH)
---
| [DONATE NOW: $28](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/984?t=27&express_lane=true&amount=28&akid=1824%2E4032634%2EO7MTjH)
---
| [DONATE NOW: $50](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/984?t=29&express_lane=true&amount=50&akid=1824%2E4032634%2EO7MTjH)
---
| [DONATE NOW: $100](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/984?t=19&express_lane=true&amount=100&akid=1824%2E4032634%2EO7MTjH)
---
| [OTHER AMOUNT](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/984?t=21&express_lane=true&akid=1824%2E4032634%2EO7MTjH)
---

---
We need to invest now to keep our majority because if the GOP takes over, they’ll derail the progress we’re making. Not only that, but they’ll actively work to take us backwards. Mitch McConnell has said that a nationwide abortion ban would be on the table if Republicans ran Washington again. Social Security and Medicare could be on the chopping block. And confirmations of President Biden’s judicial nominees would come to a screeching halt. All together, a GOP Senate would work to rip away more and more of our freedoms and threaten more and more of our democracy. Jon Tester and Sherrod Brown fight from the heart for working people across the country because they understand the challenges families face. Jon Tester’s always reminded me of my big brothers (not just because of the flat top haircut). He’s tough as nails, he’s got a heart bigger than the state of Montana, and he centers every decision on the needs of working people — because he understands those needs down to his bones. He’s the only working farmer in the Senate, and he and his wife, Sharla, work on the same land that his grandparents tended more than 100 years ago. He is the best person to represent Montanans and working families in Congress. And he gets things done. He led the effort to pass the PACT Act, providing care and benefits to service members exposed to toxins. Now he’s working to make sure the law delivers results. Sherrod Brown grew up in Mansfield, Ohio, in a community of ironworkers, carpenters, autoworkers, and electricians, spending summers working on his family's farm. He’s seen manufacturing jobs shipped overseas because of trade deals benefiting giant corporations. And he’s dedicated his life to fighting for the dignity of work. It’s a pretty basic idea: hard work should pay off for everyone. Whether you punch a time clock or swipe a badge, earn a salary or make tips, are raising children or caring for an aging parent, Sherrod fights for you. As Chair of the Banking Committee, he takes on the big banks and fights to make our economy work for everyone, not just Wall Street CEOs. [**Jon Tester and Sherrod Brown will keep making us proud if we keep their voices in Washington. But Republicans are going all out to defeat them. Can you help fight back by splitting a donation of $28 or any amount between Jon Tester, Sherrod Brown, and our re-election campaign?**](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/984?amount=28&t=15&akid=1824%2E4032634%2EO7MTjH) Thanks for being a part of this, Elizabeth
| | | |
---
| [DONATE](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/984?t=13&akid=1824%2E4032634%2EO7MTjH)
---

name: Salinas HQ
subject: BIG NEWS: Andrea officially filed for re-election!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Andrea Salinas for Oregon
body:
Last year, Andrea ran for Congress to fight for a stronger future for Oregon. And in her first nine months in Congress, she’s fought to expand access to healthcare, end the epidemic of gun violence, safeguard reproductive freedoms, bring jobs back to OR-06, and so much more. But there’s still more work to be done.

| | Year after year, millions of people cast their votes on everything from ballot initiatives to state legislative races to presidential elections. This wouldn’t be possible without the truly amazing poll workers who commit to making our elections run smoothly. These workers buckle down for long shifts and complete extensive training to help our democracy thrive, even as extremists attempt to undermine their critical work. **So as we think about what we’re thankful for, we want to express our whole-hearted appreciation for election workers. ** **[This Thanksgiving Week, will you join us in saying you’re grateful for our dedicated election workers?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/73699002/439375110/-2118555508?contactdata=rce/NqFebX1mvd6JVOJh+Y/zWBQOsukUV0FCS7t6rWFaB5hGqmwwW0tHBkZ+hOEneTCh/s7n0nlnxpn/X+CoQYy77kuBYK9Yuh5Y1fNiJ2mZyyw/q2vFXo0CojoW104w&refcode=bsc_emm_ffa_20231120_e_electionworkers_v1s2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS84ODQ1MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyNmEwMTI4Yi1lZjg3LWVlMTEtODkyNS0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=n2jsvG1QP6MS86y3U8s4g653q_GsiFYjofX77O4xc0w=&emci=d87bd883-ef87-ee11-8925-00224832e811&emdi=26a0128b-ef87-ee11-8925-00224832e811&ceid=1037277)** | [SAY THANKS](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/73699003/439375111/-2118555508?contactdata=rce/NqFebX1mvd6JVOJh+Y/zWBQOsukUV0FCS7t6rWFaB5hGqmwwW0tHBkZ+hOEneTCh/s7n0nlnxpn/X+CoQYy77kuBYK9Yuh5Y1fNiJ2mZyyw/q2vFXo0CojoW104w&refcode=bsc_emm_ffa_20231120_e_electionworkers_v1s2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS84ODQ1MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyNmEwMTI4Yi1lZjg3LWVlMTEtODkyNS0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=n2jsvG1QP6MS86y3U8s4g653q_GsiFYjofX77O4xc0w=&emci=d87bd883-ef87-ee11-8925-00224832e811&emdi=26a0128b-ef87-ee11-8925-00224832e811&ceid=1037277)

As we get ready for Thanksgiving, many poll workers are working through the holiday in Georgia to prepare for the upcoming runoff elections. We value them and their contributions, and we’ll keep working to make sure they have the resources they need to run accessible elections.
[**If you’re with us, sign onto Fair Fight Action’s card today to say you’re thankful for election workers in Georgia and across the country.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/73699004/439375112/-1165345585?contactdata=rce/NqFebX1mvd6JVOJh+Y/zWBQOsukUV0FCS7t6rWFaB5hGqmwwW0tHBkZ+hOEneTCh/s7n0nlnxpn/X+CoQYy77kuBYK9Yuh5Y1fNiJ2mZyyw/q2vFXo0CojoW104w&refcode=bsc_emm_ffa_20231120_e_electionworkers_v1s2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS84ODQ1MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyNmEwMTI4Yi1lZjg3LWVlMTEtODkyNS0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=n2jsvG1QP6MS86y3U8s4g653q_GsiFYjofX77O4xc0w=&emci=d87bd883-ef87-ee11-8925-00224832e811&emdi=26a0128b-ef87-ee11-8925-00224832e811&ceid=1037277)

clean_body: We want to make sure poll workers know just how much we appreciate them. | | | Year after year, millions of people cast their votes on everything from ballot initiatives to state legislative races to presidential elections. This wouldn’t be possible without the truly amazing poll workers who commit to making our elections run smoothly. These workers buckle down for long shifts and complete extensive training to help our democracy thrive, even as extremists attempt to undermine their critical work. So as we think about what we’re thankful for, we want to express our whole-hearted appreciation for election workers. | As we get ready for Thanksgiving, many poll workers are working through the holiday in Georgia to prepare for the upcoming runoff elections. We value them and their contributions, and we’ll keep working to make sure they have the resources they need to run accessible elections. —The Fair Fight Action Team = | * 1270 Caroline Street | Suite D120-430 | Atlanta, GA 30307 ©2023 Fair Fight Action - All rights reserved. name: Gretchen Whitmer
subject: Say thank you to the Democratic majorities who are bringing progress to Michigan
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Fight Like Hell PAC**
body:
We’re getting things done for Michiganders |
#
---
Team, Today, I signed a piece of legislation into law that will go a long way toward preventing domestic violence and improving public safety. Let me be clear: This would not have been possible without this amazing grassroots team. You knocked on doors, chipped in, and showed up to elect Democrats up and down the ballot in Michigan in 2022. And the opportunity to sign this bill today would not have occurred without Michigan Democrats working tirelessly to pass legislation that will make a meaningful difference for families all across Michigan. So pat yourself on the back — you deserve it! **[And say thank you to our Democratic majorities who are making Michigan a better place to live and raise a family.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/73699050/439375274/2074461726?refcode=AU_FLHP_EG_GEN_GEN_AC90_20231120_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDY0NjIiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYTVjMGUwYzItZWY4Ny1lZTExLTg5MjUtMDAyMjQ4MzJlODExIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=hIyqOdofyZOY97M24RgpaClPPtuapQ4Lrj7TQUMCN54=&emci=9ab1de6d-c187-ee11-8925-00224832e811&emdi=a5c0e0c2-ef87-ee11-8925-00224832e811&ceid=1028753)** | [SAY THANK YOU](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/73699051/439375275/2074461726?refcode=AU_FLHP_EG_GEN_GEN_AC90_20231120_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDY0NjIiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYTVjMGUwYzItZWY4Ny1lZTExLTg5MjUtMDAyMjQ4MzJlODExIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=hIyqOdofyZOY97M24RgpaClPPtuapQ4Lrj7TQUMCN54=&emci=9ab1de6d-c187-ee11-8925-00224832e811&emdi=a5c0e0c2-ef87-ee11-8925-00224832e811&ceid=1028753)

Survivors of domestic violence can often feel like no one has their back, but I want them to know that **they are not alone.**
We hear you. We stand with you. And we support you every step of the way.
We’re getting things done here in Michigan, and I can’t wait to share more with you soon.
Thank you,
Gretchen
Fight Like Hell PAC

Lansing, MI 48901

name: Tom Malinowski
subject: A personal note
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Districts for Democracy
body:
| | Dear friends, I launched Districts for Democracy this year to stop the book-banning and culture war crusades that right-wing groups were trying to impose on our public schools. I’m delighted to report that with your help, we succeeded. We were able to support over 300 school board candidates in the most contested races in New Jersey, and two-thirds of them won. I’m sure you heard political pundits predict that so-called “parental rights” candidates — the ones who have been pushing for book bans and forcibly outing LGBT youth across the country — would make even more gains in this November’s elections. But in New Jersey and across the country, the opposite happened.[ We demonstrated that when voters know where candidates stand on these issues, they will choose the ones who support freedom of expression and respect for the rights of all students.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/73699074/439375307/-1533047840?refcode=EM_NBI_DFD_20231120_FR_NoteFromTom1.3_0_Tom_NA_NA&refcode2=e8f17e05-f087-ee11-8925-00224832e811&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82MzYzOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlOGYxN2UwNS1mMDg3LWVlMTEtODkyNS0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=cd1lGQ6ekCQ4VK70t-n142OCWbqOsyVn75CjqAb0cFo=&emci=de3ae167-ed87-ee11-8925-00224832e811&emdi=e8f17e05-f087-ee11-8925-00224832e811&ceid=19214294) One of our biggest races was in Bernards Township, where a MAGA-dominated school board made national news by canceling a high school sociology textbook because, believe it or not, it was slightly more critical of QAnon than of Antifa. The anti-book-banning candidates we backed there swept all three contested seats, winning back control of the Board. In Westwood, a board majority that had opposed the state’s sex ed standards and pushed a policy that led to the removal of pride flags lost to candidates who promised a return to normality and equitable treatment for all. In Hunterdon County, we won some extremely tight races — in Raritan Township by 31 votes out of almost 7,000 cast; and in West Amwell by just 4 votes. In these and several other razor-close contests around the state, we used the resources you provided to contact voters directly to tell them which candidates would oppose extreme policies like book banning, and I’m confident our efforts made the difference. This fight is far from over. MAGA extremists still won some races and are not going to give up easily. Taking over our public school system — or driving parents away from it — remains central to their goal of undermining democracy in America. After all, it is in our schools that we learn the basic skills of critical thinking that help us distinguish between facts and conspiracy theories. It is in our schools that we first encounter and learn to respect people who are different from us. Defending public schools must, therefore, be central to our strategy as well. In this election, we demonstrated that when we stand up for freedom of expression and against divisive culture war policies, most voters will agree with us, even in very conservative areas. I hope what we proved on a small scale in New Jersey will be applied on a much larger stage in 2024. Thank you again for making this possible and for your commitment to all the work still to come. Tom
---
* * *
| Districts for Democracy will work one school board, legislative, and congressional district at a time to recruit and support candidates committed to freedom of inquiry, thought, and speech for everyone. We want to keep the culture wars out of our schools, return school board elections to their nonpartisan roots, and keep our schools and universities focused on their central mission: helping our kids become educated and engaged citizens of these United States.

---

_**"The problem is that Democrats' 2024 Senate challenges go far beyond West Virginia. They face such a starkly unfavorable map that, if things even go somewhat poorly for the party, they could fall into a deep Senate hole for years to come."**_
Fellow Conservative,

With Joe Manchin out, the Senate runs directly through Montana. And this means that we have our work cut out for ourselves. **[Let me give you a couple of reasons why Joe Manchin's retirement announcement elevates our race to the top spot:](http://links.conservativesdefendingfreedom.com/u/click?_t=91d01aa68bbe4105984439520ca0f0af&_m=fa6160e2710b4ed0a6bc65ac55215ee9&_e=rLEzuH_OFuXnLksyYWKLnOCekuL-tBmTxCzN3-GJE0OfKJrzqJxx8KE9BTIlVm0DygY20dUu-feLvmjkjmVzcTWZ9eOreWM70bkYbxGQxkkwpQ6PBvN5VhU_idPcRWqzOQrhk6wOxKEpJcM0CDUZFiUjTDO3d29QMdQAv3ACN7ijTO5QwxUWa1loc_RMpxjF9zoHdc_8nZpX866ePqDG5JNxrYkeywWHJNt408zNK9u_AU4Lbj9Q2NtxPn3yQ-QW3_yIGgwkkzsXPAK8JItCElcWXSZrQ0evlI7Os_C3RNbrMfstN4O5SkqL2chzRp3FBDvmGvE-QlVvMdaf9Q8fRdvdVNT83z5CNzP5zPXg5MOkaMu7AG3Tr0RTlfPXhNsn9liquly7qg_5HiWul_J8ZQ%3D%3D)**
* Chuck Schumer no longer has to decide where to put his resources–they're coming to Montana. With Joe Manchin out of the race, Schumer will all but write off West Virginia, ceding the seat to Republicans. Manchin's retirement will likely free up between $25-$50 million in defense funds for the Democrats. **[[link to donate]](http://links.conservativesdefendingfreedom.com/u/click?_t=91d01aa68bbe4105984439520ca0f0af&_m=fa6160e2710b4ed0a6bc65ac55215ee9&_e=rLEzuH_OFuXnLksyYWKLnOCekuL-tBmTxCzN3-GJE0OfKJrzqJxx8KE9BTIlVm0DygY20dUu-feLvmjkjmVzcTWZ9eOreWM70bkYbxGQxkkwpQ6PBvN5VhU_idPcRWqzOQrhk6wOxKEpJcM0CDUZFiUjTDO3d29QMdQAv3ACN7ijTO5QwxUWa1loc_RMpxjF9zoHdc_8nZpX866ePqDG5JNxrYkeywWHJNt408zNK9u_AU4Lbj9Q2NtxPn3yQ-QW_XiZ2mJjtEeiBxDGSLQjHhzBZHd2iFNBtyICCKF8SkEGWrxHgBf7WFN2Kh1dsffTZ5vFaGb3c-YS3Wl_F-tPD5eA7KMTEWy7DmQqeS_Lxp7Qq3ZJ1f3Q9SGxQ-r8OMkVqgm6DBlqbixr-vWhl8ELIw%3D%3D)**

* Schumer won't have to persuade wealthy coastal donors into donating to a Manchin reelection effort. Instead of choosing between protecting Manchin or Tester - or contributing to both candidates - they'll be donating the maximum amount only to Tester. Coastal donors have helped Tester shatter Montana fundraising records each quarter, accumulating $15 million in donations since January 1st. I need help catching up because, to be frank, we haven't brought in a fraction of this from our online community. [**[link to donate]**](http://links.conservativesdefendingfreedom.com/u/click?_t=91d01aa68bbe4105984439520ca0f0af&_m=fa6160e2710b4ed0a6bc65ac55215ee9&_e=rLEzuH_OFuXnLksyYWKLnOCekuL-tBmTxCzN3-GJE0OfKJrzqJxx8KE9BTIlVm0DygY20dUu-feLvmjkjmVzcTWZ9eOreWM70bkYbxGQxkkwpQ6PBvN5VhU_idPcRWqzOQrhk6wOxKEpJcM0CDUZFiUjTDO3d29QMdQAv3ACN7ijTO5QwxUWa1loc_RMpxjF9zoHdc_8nZpX866ePqDG5JNxrYkeywWHJNt408zNK9u_AU4Lbj9Q2NtxPn3yQ-QWCkS224t0Tj596FJqQf-hwwbn00wWXcX0rEkJ92aia2y7KrF8HHcykcvABATVlyySDVjGDmhPyHw472ciOZPWU-VkBsa2p9lVBytxmyyxgeuFCUGsJoHR_2ltQOPr3AYabKrN09tB_dERNzpsesAG_g%3D%3D)

* The other Democrat incumbents up for reelection this year are from states Biden won in 2020. That doesn't necessarily mean Republicans don't have a shot to win these races, but the incumbents aren't as likely to need as much money to win as my opponent will.

All of these things, taken together, mean that Jon Tester is about to become the recipient of some MAJOR cash. And while that alone doesn't guarantee he'll find a way to extend his nearly quarter-century political career, he might be able to buy (_**read lie**_) his way to reelection if he raises $200,000,000+.

**[I need your help. Sincerely.](http://links.conservativesdefendingfreedom.com/u/click?_t=91d01aa68bbe4105984439520ca0f0af&_m=fa6160e2710b4ed0a6bc65ac55215ee9&_e=rLEzuH_OFuXnLksyYWKLnOCekuL-tBmTxCzN3-GJE0OfKJrzqJxx8KE9BTIlVm0DygY20dUu-feLvmjkjmVzcTWZ9eOreWM70bkYbxGQxkkwpQ6PBvN5VhU_idPcRWqzOQrhk6wOxKEpJcM0CDUZFiUjTDO3d29QMdQAv3ACN7ijTO5QwxUWa1loc_RMpxjF9zoHdc_8nZpX866ePqDG5JNxrYkeywWHJNt408zNK9u_AU4Lbj9Q2NtxPn3yQ-QWxdsNWUoY7SK1vXc9QM8Asfc4G2pw_hCchkoV2LwfCEYJmqKnsX55VNxk6sDpV0jHsTIBj1ISslycKLFPoHnF4JO7wlC9fh-DJfUnRB-HTT8v0JDKF1lM8xEykyjyBhvRXTN0P3g2iArkB5K5HD1bOQ%3D%3D)** I may not be a household name but feel free to google me, my race, or my opponent's name, and you'll see the endless articles written about our race this year. **It's going to be a big.**

We have everything going for us except one thing: **money.**

1. Running against a liberal incumbent in Trump country: **check**
2. Endorsements from Montana's conservative elected officials: **check**
3. Strong grassroots support here in Montana: **check**
4. Cash to compete: **we're not there yet**

I'm hopeful you'll see this note today and decide **[it's time to contribute.](http://links.conservativesdefendingfreedom.com/u/click?_t=91d01aa68bbe4105984439520ca0f0af&_m=fa6160e2710b4ed0a6bc65ac55215ee9&_e=rLEzuH_OFuXnLksyYWKLnOCekuL-tBmTxCzN3-GJE0OfKJrzqJxx8KE9BTIlVm0DygY20dUu-feLvmjkjmVzcTWZ9eOreWM70bkYbxGQxkkwpQ6PBvN5VhU_idPcRWqzOQrhk6wOxKEpJcM0CDUZFiUjTDO3d29QMdQAv3ACN7ijTO5QwxUWa1loc_RMpxjF9zoHdc_8nZpX866ePqDG5JNxrYkeywWHJNt408zNK9u_AU4Lbj9Q2NtxPn3yQ-QW3alUCxhev6q1SjhWXo2waAsfgyrGNXH8kFQ1yKkdlBoWVoJ-jzuZY2VbahWRAcqqlJlIheYO-Ur5cc4NHbbbBghI78gJrKtaNlr2P9p3KVgGu4AUbOh235T8KxDvoJPDjc_uo2VaUxsOYtPnBqLyYQ%3D%3D)** In 2024, as goes Montana, so goes the Senate.

clean_body: We have everything going for us except one thing: money. | | "The problem is that Democrats' 2024 Senate challenges go far beyond West Virginia. They face such a starkly unfavorable map that, if things even go somewhat poorly for the party, they could fall into a deep Senate hole for years to come." Fellow Conservative, With Joe Manchin out, the Senate runs directly through Montana. And this means that we have our work cut out for ourselves. Chuck Schumer no longer has to decide where to put his resources–they're coming to Montana. With Joe Manchin out of the race, Schumer will all but write off West Virginia, ceding the seat to Republicans. Manchin's retirement will likely free up between $25-$50 million in defense funds for the Democrats. [[link to donate]]() Schumer won't have to persuade wealthy coastal donors into donating to a Manchin reelection effort. Instead of choosing between protecting Manchin or Tester - or contributing to both candidates - they'll be donating the maximum amount only to Tester. Coastal donors have helped Tester shatter Montana fundraising records each quarter, accumulating $15 million in donations since January 1st. I need help catching up because, to be frank, we haven't brought in a fraction of this from our online community. [[link to donate]]() The other Democrat incumbents up for reelection this year are from states Biden won in 2020. That doesn't necessarily mean Republicans don't have a shot to win these races, but the incumbents aren't as likely to need as much money to win as my opponent will. All of these things, taken together, mean that Jon Tester is about to become the recipient of some MAJOR cash. And while that alone doesn't guarantee he'll find a way to extend his nearly quarter-century political career, he might be able to buy (read lie) his way to reelection if he raises $200,000,000+. I may not be a household name but feel free to google me, my race, or my opponent's name, and you'll see the endless articles written about our race this year. It's going to be a big. We have everything going for us except one thing: money. 1. Running against a liberal incumbent in Trump country: check 2. Endorsements from Montana's conservative elected officials: check 3. Strong grassroots support here in Montana: check 4. Cash to compete: we're not there yet I'm hopeful you'll see this note today and decide In 2024, as goes Montana, so goes the Senate. For America's Future, Tim Sheehy Former Navy SEAL Republican Candidate for U.S. Senate | " "I SERVED AS A NAVY SEAL, BUILT ONE OF THE LARGEST AERIAL FIREFIGHTING COMPANIES IN AMERICA CREATING OVER 200 MONTANA JOBS, AND STARTED A VETERAN-LED CATTLE RANCHING AND FEEDING OPERATION. WHETHER IT WAS IN WAR OR BUSINESS, I SEE PROBLEMS AND SOLVE THEM. AMERICA NEEDS CONSERVATIVE LEADERS WHO LOVE OUR COUNTRY. I'M RUNNING FOR THE U.S. SENATE BECAUSE OUR CAMPAIGN IS ABOUT SERVICE, DUTY, AND COUNTRY -- NOT POLITICS AS USUAL." * | PAID FOR BY TIM SHEEHY FOR MONTANA Tim Sheehy is a former Navy SEAL. clean_body: To check off all our boxes, we’re going to need grassroots supporters like you to be the driving force that pushes Democrats to victory. | | | | | ---|--- 🚨 15 Days Until Our Next Special Election 🚨 15 days, that’s all we have left to make an impact before our next special elections in Florida and Minnesota. And President Joe Biden is calling on each member of this grassroots team to support our movement to elect state Democrats: Republicans have funneled millions into state-level races, making every special election an even tougher battle to win. We’re heeding President Biden’s call, and we need you to stand with him, too – there’s a hell of a lot more to do. Here’s our to-do list to secure Democratic victory in special elections:: Strategize with our candidates Mobilize resources to push our state Democrats across the finish line Invest in key races to ensure every vote counts Engage voters through door-knocking and phone banking To check off all our boxes, we’re going to need grassroots supporters like you, to be the driving force that pushes Democrats to victory. | If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process immediately. | | | | | Thank you for being a crucial part of this team, Team DLCC | Democratic Legislative Campaign Committee 1225 Eye Street NW Suite 1250, Washington, DC 20005 Paid for by DLCC PAC. subject: How to talk politics at Thanksgiving
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Busse for Montana, PO Box 8537 Kalispell, MT 59904, Democrat
body:
Ryan Busse puts principles over politics. If you’d like to unsubscribe, click here. [https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/KuMB1syMs0W9O3v5MuCvqQ2?unsubscribedata=uuPU%2BPZYxbmQoj5dzLLUwZlPprwidlwCI%2FBaSmpineLdqnUG%2BhojGUVkRjOCqMFr5tC8R8VA2qLDOx9Hi5uSZZWN7Eg82rX8xcJPRYACrg9augWpBrPyEAXyANEiCCEZSToQGIJisNQBv7%2FWnUlb43gAZoGOReazQ9St6DmcRZ25eufNjxX4fBWfBYFIFqhm3Y8Vd8Sl8UsthOtug%2BTlTcm%2FPUNasy4tdbk1KkifPRtoyz22E8nWTxnZmz%2FKYwYYorHJTdkenuQJ2f2s584uNfNzBQ9j38utg2tT%2FMydBphGQR2OzrLqibBn27XNyl2v%2FBeHOm8ONPl41TaItMbPPojIF02EvrL64k5VOGi9MakkYDY4p9ha3sRsgQRZBnuLd9HLNdSw%2BPGB4Vgl3WHgjxDI2z4Tz543%2BmHSNddPTXW5jd5uYvulEceEEdGWWCWE]

# [#]
Hello from Team Busse!

This isn’t a typical political email begging for campaign cash. Instead, as we approach the holidays, we’re sharing some practical messaging advice for Democrats from Ryan Busse as he runs to become Montana’s next governor.

I’ve coached candidates and elected leaders on political messaging for more than 17 years, and Ryan is one of the strongest communicators I’ve ever worked with.

That means I was listening carefully when Ryan appeared live on a conservative talk radio call-in show the other day. He spent an hour taking tough questions from callers who regularly listen to conservative talk radio.

His thoughtful, clear answers, and the respectful way he handled questions, was so effective that we want to pass along some of his messaging tips for talking politics with your feisty, conservative relatives who may be sitting around your dining room table this holiday season.

1.
Don’t
Take
Their
Bait.
https://youtu.be/JHnL3JogMC0
[https://youtu.be/JHnL3JogMC0]

This
caller
wants
to
lure
Ryan
into
a
debate
over
identity
politics.
When
Democrats
play
defense
on
identity
politics,
we
often
lose
sight
of
the
bigger
political
picture:
winning
elections.
The
caller
asks
about
a
transgender
woman
who
wears
a
dress.

“This
is
the
stuff
that’s
being
taught
in
public
schools,”
the
caller
falsely
claims,
then
adds:
“Is
this
individual
a
man
or
a
woman?”
Of
course
Ryan
believes
in
the
freedom
for
all
people
to
be
themselves
,
with
respect
and
dignity.
But
note
how
Ryan
turns
the
focus
back
on
the
caller’s
mean-spirited
intent:
*
RYAN:
I
really
don’t
appreciate
you
taking
cheap
shots
at
people,
trying
to
stoke
some
culture
war
hatred.

Ryan’s
response
is
firm

If

See

2.
Wedge
Issues?
Wedge

https://youtu.be/gfHaMcrTwqA

This

about

many

First,

Did

[your

playing

on

*

...And

[their

is

So

Now

RYAN:
I

to

on

Now

clean_body: Ryan Busse puts principles over politics. If you’d like to unsubscribe, click here. [] [#] Hello from Team Busse! This isn’t a typical political email begging for campaign cash. Instead, as we approach the holidays, we’re sharing some practical messaging advice for Democrats from Ryan Busse as he runs to become Montana’s next governor. I’ve coached candidates and elected leaders on political messaging for more than 17 years, and Ryan is one of the strongest communicators I’ve ever worked with. That means I was listening carefully when Ryan appeared live on a conservative talk radio call-in show the other day. He spent an hour taking tough questions from callers who regularly listen to conservative talk radio. His thoughtful, clear answers, and the respectful way he handled questions, was so effective that we want to pass along some of his messaging tips for talking politics with your feisty, conservative relatives who may be sitting around your dining room table this holiday season. 1. Don’t Take Their Bait. [] This caller wants to lure Ryan into a debate over identity politics. When Democrats play defense on identity politics, we often lose sight of the bigger political picture: winning elections. The caller asks about a transgender woman who wears a dress. “This is the stuff that’s being taught in public schools,” the caller falsely claims, then adds: “Is this individual a man or a woman?” Of course Ryan believes in the freedom for all people to be themselves , with respect and dignity. But note how Ryan turns the focus back on the caller’s mean-spirited intent: RYAN: I really don’t appreciate you taking cheap shots at people, trying to stoke some culture war hatred. Ryan’s response is firm but respectful. But rather than keep the focus on identity politics, Ryan sees an opening to correct the caller’s false claim about what’s being taught in public schools: RYAN: My kids have been in public school. I still have a sophomore in public school. They’re not reaching this stuff. They are teaching people to help people instead of hurt people, and I’m unclear what decent, reasonable Montanan thinks that it’s a bad idea to be decent and respectful to people. It should be a pretty dang low bar. That’s what I stand for. If you want to take up your day by worrying about these made-up culture wars, go for it. But I’m just not down with that. See what happened there? What was supposed to be a question and an answer about identity politics ended as a correction... and a scolding. 2. Wedge Issues? Wedge Back! [] This caller feels strongly about the issue of abortion. Her strategy is to wedge her anti-abortion perspective of this complex issue by using hyperbolic, graphic language—a tactic many on the Right use to put anyone who believes in the freedom of choice on defense. First, note how Ryan reframes the issue right off the top, without falling into the trap of answering the caller’s wedge-issue question: CALLER: Where’s your limit on killing babies? RYAN: So you asked a question about women’s health care... Then Ryan uses equally effective language to wedge the issue back, harnessing the power of his new frame: women and their health care: RYAN: I believe that the Governor of Montana has no right to be in the doctor’s office with the women of this state, creepily looking at them, telling them what they can and can’t do with their health care decisions. It’s not my decision. It should be a doctor’s, and a woman’s, and their god’s decision, between all of them—it’s not mine. And it’s definitely not Greg Gianforte’s. And this weirdness—that he wants to get into doctors’ offices with women and tell them what they can and can’t do? Sorry, I’m not down with that either. Note how Ryan flips the script, accurately framing his opponent, billionaire Greg Gianforte, as a creepy, anti-choice religious zealot who has plans to invade women’s privacy. 3. Pivot, Then Play Offense [] We all know people like this caller, who tries to shame Ryan into applying for a property tax rebate from the State of Montana. Though this particular issue is specific to Montana, the wider pivot-then-play-offense strategy is important for progressives and Democrats to use everywhere. First, some context: Gianforte purposely made it difficult for Montana homeowners to apply for their property tax rebates this year, after the governor and his Republican supermajority in our state legislature jacked up property taxes—something most Republicans claim they oppose. Applying for this year’s rebate was such a difficult process (requiring homeowners to track down their “geocodes”) that tens of thousands of Montanans didn’t even bother. This caller persistently tries to embarrass Ryan for claiming his rebate, absurdly suggesting that Ryan should have given his money back to the government because he objects to Gianforte’s rebate process. But note how Ryan changes the subject, or pivots: CALLER: Did you send [your rebate] back to the revenue department or did you take it to the food bank to donate it? RYAN: There were 64,500 people in the State of Montana that were due that rebate and they did not get it... Pivoting, though, is only half the strategy. Watch how Ryan once again turns the tables. Instead of playing defense on a caller’s half-baked quest to make Ryan feel guilty, Ryan plays offense—which we believe Democrats don’t do often enough. RYAN: ...And the reason they didn't get [their rebates] is because there was all of this red tape that was placed on that rebate by the Republicans and the Governor. They know what your geocode is. They know what your address is. They know where you live. Why? Because they send you tax bills. So why are we penalizing all of these people in this state who are owed that rebate? Now watch how Ryan keeps playing defense, respectfully redirecting the shame back onto the caller for defending a Republican tax increase: * RYAN: I don’t know why you would want to defend a system that makes it harder on people to get the money they’re owed, and then pick on people when they take the money they’re owed. This is their money. Now if you’re so worried about people being owed money then I think you ought be plenty worried about this property tax increase being laid over the top of everybody very purposefully. See how much more effective this strategy is? Just ask the University of Montana Grizzlies: You score more touchdowns playing offense! If you’re interested in listening to Ryan’s full radio interview, we invite you to do so here: [] Put these tips to work when you find yourself debating politics in the weeks ahead. And from all of us at Team Busse, have a safe and happy Thanksgiving! Aaron Murphy Advisor, Busse for Montana PS: Though I said I wasn’t going to beg for campaign cash, I’d be remiss not to ask for it. If you believe we need a tough candidate like Ryan fighting for Democracy in Montana, >> please consider giving what you can by clicking here. [] << Your generous financial support is paramount to Ryan’s victory. [] Ryan is running to fight for freedom, fairness, and common sense. Meanwhile, liars, cheats, billionaires, and fascists are unraveling American democracy before our eyes. We should be terrified of their anti-freedom agenda. Greg Gianforte is the problem. He’s already raised your taxes, ignored the high cost of housing, lied to Montanans, and even went jet-setting in Italy while Montanans had to deal with record-breaking floods. He’s just getting started. Ryan is ready to deliver “a new brand for Montana,” and protect the Last Best Place from Greg Gianforte’s dangerous, failed leadership. | | Peter, The damage caused by the fire under the 10 freeway at a critical choke point in Downtown Los Angeles has me thinking about how so many of our essential thoroughfares are one bad accident or natural disaster away from creating absolute havoc in our communities. Just rerouting the school buses to avoid that stretch of highway was a huge logistical nightmare — we need to make meaningful investments in our infrastructure _before_ things like this happen to ensure we're prepared with ample options and detours to keep our community moving. Whether it's the Santa Ana winds during fire season, El Niño, mudslides from flash floods, or the side effects of unmaintained infrastructure — we need to start being more proactive. **It's no secret that our basic infrastructure is failing and we have to take bold action before we fall behind.** [**If you're ready to send a champion for a robust infrastructure to Congress, help get me there by making a $5 contribution to my campaign.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/73699812/439377972/1183499175?refcode=2023-11-20-infrastructure&recurring=&amount=5&amounts=10,30,50,100,200&refcode2=1c88deeb-f087-ee11-8925-00224832e811&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDQxNjIiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMWM4OGRlZWItZjA4Ny1lZTExLTg5MjUtMDAyMjQ4MzJlODExIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=fCsShgmjRDEXniQa1s9TZB5x79DJtWAvFVv64iAY--4=) [Donate Now](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/73699813/439377973/1183499175?refcode=2023-11-20-infrastructure&recurring=&amount=5&amounts=10,30,50,100,200&refcode2=1c88deeb-f087-ee11-8925-00224832e811&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDQxNjIiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMWM4OGRlZWItZjA4Ny1lZTExLTg5MjUtMDAyMjQ4MzJlODExIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=fCsShgmjRDEXniQa1s9TZB5x79DJtWAvFVv64iAY--4=) In my current role as a local leader, we made historic progress on infrastructure by passing the largest school infrastructure bond in the country to modernize our schools. **Congress must capitalize on the momentum of the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act bill to fix our roads, expand digital access, and develop new, environmentally friendly public transportation options while creating jobs and addressing inequity.** And I know how to do it. In Congress, I will fight for:
* Improved public transportation systems and hubs to decrease our dependence on cars and alleviate traffic.
* More green energy and transportation infrastructure development.
* Equitable investment of transportation dollars to create jobs and support historically disadvantaged communities.
* Ensuring that our region gets ample funding to support our densely-populated communities with the infrastructure they need.

clean_body: LA traffic is rough, but we can make it better. | | | | Peter, The damage caused by the fire under the 10 freeway at a critical choke point in Downtown Los Angeles has me thinking about how so many of our essential thoroughfares are one bad accident or natural disaster away from creating absolute havoc in our communities. Just rerouting the school buses to avoid that stretch of highway was a huge logistical nightmare — we need to make meaningful investments in our infrastructure before things like this happen to ensure we're prepared with ample options and detours to keep our community moving. Whether it's the Santa Ana winds during fire season, El Niño, mudslides from flash floods, or the side effects of unmaintained infrastructure — we need to start being more proactive. It's no secret that our basic infrastructure is failing and we have to take bold action before we fall behind. = = In my current role as a local leader, we made historic progress on infrastructure by passing the largest school infrastructure bond in the country to modernize our schools. Congress must capitalize on the momentum of the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act bill to fix our roads, expand digital access, and develop new, environmentally friendly public transportation options while creating jobs and addressing inequity. And I know how to do it. In Congress, I will fight for: Improved public transportation systems and hubs to decrease our dependence on cars and alleviate traffic. More green energy and transportation infrastructure development. Equitable investment of transportation dollars to create jobs and support historically disadvantaged communities. Ensuring that our region gets ample funding to support our densely-populated communities with the infrastructure they need. = More soon, Nick | Express Donate: If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution instantly: | = | = | = | = ---|---|---|--- | | | Paid for by Nick Melvoin for Congress - FEC ID #C00831289 |

name: Sherrod Brown
subject: this race is certain to be my toughest yet
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Gillibrand for Senate
body:
I need your help [https://als-totem.s3.amazonaws.com/core/spacer.gif] Friends, I've run a lot of tough races in my life, but this race is certain to be my toughest yet. [https://als-totem.s3.amazonaws.com/core/spacer.gif]
CNN says I'm one of the most vulnerable senators on the ballot. Politico says Ohio could decide Senate control. A Trump-allied dark money group is already running attack ads against me. And now, my wealthy GOP opponents are holding country club fundraisers and writing their campaigns multimillion dollar checks to try and buy this seat.
Without critical grassroots support from folks like you, I simply cannot fight back and compete. That's why I've set an ambitious $100,000 mid-month fundraising goal and have to ask you to split $5 or more right now between my campaign and Kirsten Gillibrand's to help me get there. Please, will you donate now? [http://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/gfs_sherrodbrown?refcode=jtk4225-bu-text&amounts=5%2C25%2C50%2C100%2C250]
Friends, I'm fighting everyday in the Senate for the Dignity of Work – all work. Whether you punch a clock or swipe a badge, earn a salary or work for tips, are raising children or caring for an aging parent, I'm fighting for you.
Meanwhile, my millionaire GOP opponents have spent their lives profiting off of a system that too often puts the bottom line over the actual needs of workers and their families. That's not what Ohioans, or Americans, deserve.
I know we can defend this seat and keep making meaningful progress together – so please, friend : Will you split a donation between my campaign and Kirsten Gillibrand's before the crucial mid-month deadline tonight at midnight? Every dollar goes a long way toward winning in Ohio and keeping the Senate blue. [http://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/gfs_sherrodbrown?refcode=jtk4225-bu-text&amounts=5%2C25%2C50%2C100%2C250]
If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation between Sherrod Brown and Kirsten Gillibrand will go through immediately:
DONATE $5 [https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/gfs_sherrodbrown?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&express_lane=true&amount=5]
DONATE $25 [https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/gfs_sherrodbrown?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&express_lane=true&amount=25]
DONATE $50 [https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/gfs_sherrodbrown?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&express_lane=true&amount=50]
ANOTHER AMOUNT [https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/gfs_sherrodbrown?amounts=5,25,50,100,250]
I can't thank you enough for your support.
Sherrod

body:
| | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/26dbc550bbc1/icymi-john-teicherts-vision-for-marylands-senate-on-fox-6682378?e=1b1fb1ad9f)
---
# In The News....
Last week, John appeared on News Nation to utilize his knowledge and experience with laser weapons in warfare to discuss the Pentagon’s use of the technology in battle. Recalling his time as Commander of Edwards Air Force Base, Teichert discussed how testing and operationalizing laser use for defensive and offensive purposes could be beneficial for national security.
Watch his interview below:
[](https://teichertformaryland.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=1ec0c6fa331e836475672e631&id=8421f75bf0&e=1b1fb1ad9f)
|
---
# On the Trail...
John had a busy week on the campaign trail! He enjoyed spending time with friends, family, and voters as he continued to share his message of leadership, not politics; here are some of the places he’s been!

# Thoughts...
On Veterans Day, General Teichert introduced his favorite veteran: his lovely wife, Melonie. He also thanked all military members, veterans, and their families for their brave service. Watch the full heartfelt video [here.](https://teichertformaryland.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=1ec0c6fa331e836475672e631&id=63517f8475&e=1b1fb1ad9f)
[](https://teichertformaryland.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=1ec0c6fa331e836475672e631&id=383a310d20&e=1b1fb1ad9f)
On Tuesday, November 14th, John Teichert shared a video sharing his thoughts on Biden’s meeting with Chinese President Xi and America’s evident weakness under the Biden Administration. Watch the full video [here. ](https://teichertformaryland.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=1ec0c6fa331e836475672e631&id=bcfd504721&e=1b1fb1ad9f)
[](https://teichertformaryland.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=1ec0c6fa331e836475672e631&id=43a2361c8a&e=1b1fb1ad9f)
On Wednesday, November 15th, John Teichert released “Enduring Another Self-Induced Crisis”, expressing his concern over the disarray and lack of leadership in Washington D.C., drastic government overspending, and the fragile economy that American families encounter every day.Read the full article [here.](https://teichertformaryland.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=1ec0c6fa331e836475672e631&id=6126712bc0&e=1b1fb1ad9f)
Thank you for joining John on the trail as he looks to become the next U.S. Senator for the State of Maryland. Make sure to check out [teichertformaryland.com](https://teichertformaryland.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=1ec0c6fa331e836475672e631&id=ad7180c8bc&e=1b1fb1ad9f) and see how you can donate, get involved, and join the mailing list!

Peter,

Our race has changed quite a bit recently, but my priorities for 2024 haven’t changed at all. With Joe Manchin now out, I’m laser-focused on:

1. **Stopping Chuck Schumer from interfering in West Virginia.**
2. **Helping Republicans across the Country win in crucial battleground states.**

[**These priorities may seem simple and obvious, but they will take A LOT of work and even more funding to make happen.**](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=b48b7a1214bf4e4286c923ea0e165bb8&_e=KVyIPUIAmjtP3F5D7p-HLCQ7Cz43IccMy9Ba8Ib1QMrcq8Vfg4drASN2rLFsNrpoebR77hCusDwtyFn-rBVCpTqztvR2kzGYMKgy6YUxRKfVYek5f9VntO7xgfSNj6CRlnUTbr9-jMIXll1frtnP90fF1oWUFz02fsi0Mcmy8IoYS1bk4abZdHxbyGQ8OMtn5BFZCWmhlwyuytZfSkUYe1Xdskvcq5W78MvjuPj98nP17ic4GQxJOyJwbroooQA1CPk0xv-E0jCiIwR71cWlJ0PJ1_x3PXTjDAryw2IHEM1Js1cnyhXJtGjZMP_jgSD4dvfd9t3z2gl_8WetuP7mu5DuIYe_-4bmBl7uX1E7Gm8iATRAa5LqrdlI9Cd-IgjeViMNMdxNfs8E_7XB49zihrSuFsIZ9cusPsgFmXSnOoU%3D)

Gov. Jim Justice is a conservative leader, successful businessman, high school basketball coach, and devoted family man. Before being elected, Gov. Justice led over 100 companies, creating thousands of jobs.

Gov. Justice has delivered on his promise of diversifying West Virginia's economy. He enacted the largest personal income tax cut in state history. As an avid outdoorsman, he vigorously defends the Second Amendment. He is passionate about mentoring young people and serves as the head coach of his local high school basketball team.

Gov. Justice and his wife, First Lady Cathy Justice, have been best friends since high school. They recently celebrated their 46th wedding anniversary. The Governor and the First Lady have two adult children and spend time spoiling four grandchildren. In addition, they have three dogs: Lucy and Ellie, Boston Terriers, and their famous Babydog, an English Bulldog.

Hi Peter,
I hope that you’re about to start a wonderful holiday season surrounded by friends and family. For me, I’m not really in the holiday spirit until my partner Shawn (by far the cook in our house) whips up his pumpkins bars! And I want to share my favorite recipe with you:

|

Now if you’re like me, you probably can’t read that! And that’s because this is a family recipe, and the bakers know exactly what they’re doing. If you’d like to try these pumpkin bars yourself, you can find the actual recipe below. _**Are there any holiday recipes that you enjoy?**_ Thank you again for being part of this movement. I’m grateful for your support and excited to see what 2024 brings. \- Eric

**Pumpkin Bars**
* 1 1⁄2 sticks of unsalted butter (softened)
* 1 15oz can of pumpkin
* 4 eggs
* 2 cups of sugar
* 2 cups of flour
* 2 tsp baking soda
* 1⁄2 tsp salt
* 2 tsp cinnamon
* 1 tsp nutmeg
* 1⁄2 tsp ground clove

1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees
2. Cream butter and sugar till fluffy
3. Mix in eggs one at a time
4. Add can of pumpkin
5. Sift dry ingredients and add to wet ingredients
6. Mix till well combined
7. Bake in 9X13 Pyrex for 25 to 30 minutes or in muffin cups for 20 to 25 minutes*

*if using a metal pan butter bottom and sides **Cream Cheese Frosting**
* 1 stick of unsalted butter (softened)
* 1 8oz package of cream cheese (softened)
* 1 tsp vanilla extract
* 1 tsp almond extract
* 4 cups of powdered sugar sifted

1. Cream the butter, sugar, vanilla extract, and almond extract till light and fluffy
2. Add cream cheese till well incorporated and smooth

---
|
---
| Paid For By Eric Sorensen for Illinois

# Congressman Cory Mills: War Veteran and National Hero Endorses JR Majewski

Peter,
War Veteran and National Hero: Congressman Cory Mills has just endorsed our campaign. This is HUGE! His efforts to save Americans in Afghanistan and Israel display a distinct representation of his character: one of service before self.
We're in this together, standing strong with leaders who aren't afraid to fight the tough fights. The America First agenda is our roadmap to making our nation great, prosperous, and respected again. With Cory in our corner, we're sending a message loud and clear: we're here to shake things up.

Patriots, the battle lines are drawn, and it's us against The Establishment. They're pouring millions into Ohio to stop our America First agenda. But we have something stronger — we have YOU. Can you chip in $10 to crush the Establishment's schemes? Your donation is a declaration that we won't be silenced or pushed around by the deep-pocketed elites.
Every dollar you contribute is a direct hit against The Establishment's manipulation and a push forward for our cause. We're not just defending Ohio; we're safeguarding the heart and soul of America. Let's show these elitists the true power of the American spirit.
Onward to Victory,

Peter,

We are officially less than a year away from Election Day. Our momentum continues to grow, and it is all because of your support. I am eternally grateful to have you by my side in this race.

But as we draw closer to 2024, it is more critical than ever that I keep meeting with voters and continue to connect with local organizations. I have said it once and I will say it again: this campaign is powered by grassroots supporters.

**Grassroots supporters like you, Peter.**

I know there is a lot happening right now. Orange County families are not immune to the rising cost of living and the threats coming from the MAGA extremists now in charge of the House.

That is why I knew I needed to reach out to see what your thoughts are and what you would like to prioritize as I travel around the district.

Having your support is critical. **CA-47 is the only Democratic-held open seat in California, and this race is already considered a “toss-up” by political experts.**

**[As we plan our strategy for the rest of the year, will you take one minute to complete our survey? clean_body: | = Peter, We are officially less than a year away from Election Day. Our momentum continues to grow, and it is all because of your support. I am eternally grateful to have you by my side in this race. But as we draw closer to 2024, it is more critical than ever that I keep meeting with voters and continue to connect with local organizations. I have said it once and I will say it again: this campaign is powered by grassroots supporters. Grassroots supporters like you, Peter. I know there is a lot happening right now. Orange County families are not immune to the rising cost of living and the threats coming from the MAGA extremists now in charge of the House. That is why I knew I needed to reach out to see what your thoughts are and what you would like to prioritize as I travel around the district. Having your support is critical. CA-47 is the only Democratic-held open seat in California, and this race is already considered a “toss-up” by political experts. | ** In unity, Joanna =** | Joanna's campaign doesn't take a dime from corporate PACs and completely relies on grassroots supporters like you! To chip in to Joanna's campaign = To learn more about Joanna, please visit: We'd hate to see you go, but you can click here if you'd like to . PAID FOR BY JOANNA WEISS FOR CONGRESS Joanna Weiss for Congress PO Box 3122 Costa Mesa, CA 92628 United States

name: Tony Evers
subject: how we can keep Wisconsin and the White House blue next year
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tony for Wisconsin
body:
Derek,

Republicans across the country have shown us that they will do whatever
they can to pass a nationwide abortion ban.

But voters have rejected extreme Republicans who want to ban abortion
because folks across the country agree that the government should not play
a role in personal health care decisions.

That’s why voters in Ohio overwhelmingly supported a ballot measure to
enshrine abortion rights in their state constitution, voters in Virginia
voted for Democratic majorities in their state legislature to protect
against abortion restrictions, and voters in Kentucky reelected Governor
Andy Beshear to another term.

And that’s why we won our race last year, and why Wisconsinites
overwhelmingly rejected an anti-abortion candidate running for the state
Supreme Court earlier this year.

Next year, we have what it takes to mobilize voters, beat Donald Trump,
and keep Wisconsin and the White House blue.

Fellow American,

As Speaker of the House, I work _**FOR**_ the people.

When I ran for this position, I made a promise to release the footage from January 6th, so Americans could see for themselves what happened that day, _rather than the opinions of the partisan January 6 Committee._ **And I am delivering on that promise.**

So, I applaud the decision to **release the footage on a public website available for ANYONE to view.**

**I firmly believe** the American people deserve **TRUTH** and transparency - [**which is why it is so critical for Republicans to retake the majority in 2024.**](http://links.makingamericathegreatest.com/u/click?_t=79fa4a3daf024a0a886c5b498ff32e32&_m=a9791efb1fc9416c822a7061dec9b962&_e=HYzABsGPr7eUctm5PfhsS6A0F2xTKlGumM35Fn-I2AZZo5NxKHbEYbKDrUDtG-t46AOWufI0H1EJ9PMTUM-k3jB4lreOMYzvcz4a9AcpaITUgbGVfDH7Yyj06a29vuVro4saATj7NnA23Rba7MVx1cWE2jQCWXUhqXkXmI5OwPbVwSAJVs5ufw0ERCgXunJjpVU7iBFNlaQqJjJOdzEVV4axRzyr3EQaYW3eSpNqfxM-MYUwxRPP9WYmvba0S9macQnfKlra6IxXhyo8JinswcDHduUlp_ZxOGidSp7vIjh70SbU5lw_xxJFUS3WhkL1xQ-yHeGO3-g6ZRjSPjqNdOj4V_2FQ5y-a078Z0Qra4SGbDYwrYN6JBBPmYXg8k-Y)

| **_SPEAKER MIKE JOHNSON_**

And I am delivering on that promise. So, I applaud the decision to release the footage on a public website available for ANYONE to view. I firmly believe the American people deserve TRUTH and transparency - For AMERICA, Mike Johnson Speaker of the House As Speaker, I want to hear from you on the top issues of the day. It's important for us to deliver results for the American people. Would you take a moment to complete my today? Your input is important to making sure we move America forward. I appreciate you taking the time to send me your input. | SPEAKER MIKE JOHNSON Speaker Johnson is a dedicated husband and father of four, as well as a constitutional law attorney who has devoted his life and career to fighting for the fundamental freedoms and traditional values that have always been a priority to the people of Louisiana. | PAID FOR BY MIKE JOHNSON FOR LOUISIANA Don't want to receive any more of our emails? We hate to see you go, but .

# ABOUT ERIC EARLY
---
Eric Early is a husband, father, attorney, businessman and son of a Marine. **Not a career politician.** Eric was raised to believe in America’s founding principles of limited government and free markets. He believes that every American should be given the opportunity to fulfill their American Dream, and his parents inspired Eric to achieve his personal goals. As the co-owner and Managing Partner of a nationally recognized law firm, Eric understands how difficult it is to start and run a successful business. As your next US Senator, Eric will work to cut overly-burdensome regulations that get in the way of entrepreneurship and job creation. Eric will stand-up to the Socialist woke interests that control Washington, DC, fight to preserve and protect our Democracy, and fight for ordinary Californians who are being left behind. He is passionate about standing with law enforcement and making our communities more safe, protecting Social Security and Medicare, fixing our crumbling roads and bridges, and restoring the SALT deduction to lower taxes and make New York more affordable. | | USE THE ADDRESS BELOW TO CONTRIBUTE BY CHECK LAWLER FOR CONGRESS PO BOX 87 SOUTH SALEM, NY 10590 GET YOUR MIKE LAWLER, NO CUOMO MERCH No New Yorker wants to call our Tappan Zee Bridge the “Governor Mario M. name: Bob Casey
subject: Greedflation
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY BOB CASEY FOR SENATE INC
body:
# [](https://go.bobcasey.com/go/181?t=1&akid=1182%2E552475%2E-jHQ-4)
Hi Peter, Bob Casey here.
We all know that prices are rising way too fast, pinching families across the Commonwealth. My office recently released a report, documenting how corporations have been padding their profits by billions at the expense of Pennsylvanians. We've documented how they are raising the prices on everyday goods far faster than their costs have gone up to produce them.
You can count on this. I'm going to be holding these companies accountable. I'm going to fight back on behalf of Pennsylvania families -- but I'm well aware that families have to deal with this every day while I tackle this problem at the federal level.
There's another way we can fight back together.
According to the New York Times, nearly two thirds of us have already started choosing some store brand products as a way to help deal with rising prices. For many families, it's been a way to cope, but let's turn it into a way to fight back. To fight back against the companies taking advantage of Pennsylvanians with excessive profits.
Have you found store brand products where the quality is still good enough for your family but the prices are lower? If you'll share your best finds, it'll help get the word out to other Pennsylvanians.
**[I want to hear from you, Peter. Share the store brand options you've found to help cope with Greedflation. Please take a moment to fill out this survey, and I'll share some of the responses with Pennsylvanians in the coming weeks.](https://go.bobcasey.com/survey/casey-email-greedflation-survey/?source=em20231120-3&t=2&akid=1182%2E552475%2E-jHQ-4)**
**[Take the Survey »](https://go.bobcasey.com/survey/casey-email-greedflation-survey/?source=em20231120-3&t=3&akid=1182%2E552475%2E-jHQ-4)**
There is lots of work to do at the federal level to stop the corporations who are prioritizing their profits over working families -- and I'm working in Washington to make corporations pay their fair share, fight unfair corporate price gouging, and take on corporate monopolies.
Over the past few years, I was proud to help pass laws like the American Rescue Plan, the CHIPS and Science Act, the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act, and the Inflation Reduction Act, which created good-paying jobs in PA and decreased PA unemployment to the lowest level on record. I firmly opposed the 2017 Republican tax bill that lowered the corporate tax rate, and then introduced legislation to make the tax code fairer for workers. I also support the Price Gouging Prevention Act, which would crack down on large corporations and bring price relief to consumers.
**[This is just the beginning, Peter. There's more work to do to put money back in the pockets of working families, but in the meantime, I hope you'll join in the effort to fight back at the grocery store, by sharing the tricks you've found to save money by purchasing store brand products.](https://go.bobcasey.com/survey/casey-email-greedflation-survey/?source=em20231120-3&t=4&akid=1182%2E552475%2E-jHQ-4)**
Thank you, and I look forward to seeing your tips and tricks.
With gratitude,
Bob Casey

* * *
To receive texts from Team Casey text TEAM to 51041. This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://go.bobcasey.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=5&akid=1182%2E552475%2E-jHQ-4)
* [Update Your Information](https://go.bobcasey.com/signup/update-your-information/)
* [Unsubscribe](https://go.bobcasey.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=6&akid=1182%2E552475%2E-jHQ-4)

To give by check:
Bob Casey for Senate
PO Box 58746
Philadelphia, PA 19102
Email us: [info@bobcasey.com](mailto:info@bobcasey.com)
PAID FOR BY BOB CASEY FOR SENATE INC.

name: Richard Blumenthal
subject: My thoughts on the Supreme Court’s new “Code of Conduct”
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Blumenthal for Connecticut
body:
| | | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/73703758/439387009/623074613?code=jtk1356-su-nd1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDM3OTQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZWQyYmRiYTctZmI4Ny1lZTExLTg5MjUtMDAyMjQ4MzJlODExIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=u-V1rL14WpQU1-w4mJeYRmhsPa2HvKHCpAd4oKCRXFc=&emci=b4b51bf6-b587-ee11-8925-00224832e811&emdi=ed2bdba7-fb87-ee11-8925-00224832e811&ceid=9329426)
---
Friend, in the face of a crisis of legitimacy, the Supreme Court has just adopted an official “Code of Conduct.”
This comes after numerous reports of egregious corruption from Justices Thomas and Alito. These offenses have been staggering and unacceptable, and they have led – in conjunction with the out-of-control, extremist decisions coming from the Court in recent years – to public trust in the Supreme Court plummeting to an all-time low.

**While this new Code of Conduct may seem like a step toward rebuilding that trust in our nation’s highest court, in reality, it is no more than a decoy. And a disappointing one at that.**
The policy leaves several key issues unresolved – the most important of which is enforcement. Who will make sure the Code of Conduct is followed? What are the penalties for refusal or failure by Justices? There is a lot of ‘devil’ in these unspecified details.

For as long as I’ve been in public service, I’ve been laser-focused on ethics and accountability for our government’s most powerful institutions – a focus that has only grown more important in the past year.

That’s why I’m working alongside my colleagues on the Senate Judiciary Committee to draft real, meaningful ethical guidelines for the Supreme Court that will ensure accountability and begin to restore trust in our judicial branch.
**On an issue as crucial as this, it’s important for me to hear from you directly about where you stand. That’s why I’m hoping for 1,000 supporters to let me know whether or not they approve of the current Supreme Court – and I’m hoping to see your response:**

[**Do you approve of the current Supreme Court? Please click here to share your response.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/73703759/439387010/2003786113?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100&code=jtk1356-su-nd1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDM3OTQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZWQyYmRiYTctZmI4Ny1lZTExLTg5MjUtMDAyMjQ4MzJlODExIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=u-V1rL14WpQU1-w4mJeYRmhsPa2HvKHCpAd4oKCRXFc=&emci=b4b51bf6-b587-ee11-8925-00224832e811&emdi=ed2bdba7-fb87-ee11-8925-00224832e811&ceid=9329426)

Thank you so much for adding your voice today.

Sincerely,
Dick
| This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.
[To unsubscribe from this email list, please click here.](https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/p3FPDNcG3USxiOP5q9s2qQ2?unsubscribedata=CxK44pdGHYBry8uKmwrW5nvA39lKnxog1p0XFVGtZKrHmAfX0P4%2B3ZxsTgrTpILgmF9UW2VSBzo0yT%2Bgt1u6Z%2F1BVc5FGCAzzseo1xThMrZeOn3%2BPP7rxepxOT1N1PKxTtFlctOJwC7kGUOubCKh0dVExtUY%2F9ZPVcZZxREXsLaCNFnaOP5YDTmCIX0WwmAdoMQFrd8sA%2FR%2BEgv3IWkULFY62tHu2sn1A6Ciu7wYa49qmn8chIphZXLkGvVHq8nWp8e2K86iHmq8SONezzPfNHoINYp7NlzIudY22iMfoTzVjET0WoYIKBATmRn920rW6qqXzZ3hFnYEW%2Ftft5Q%2FsaW80ojsJxvhakzU0p6WNrheRMXq0XQmg5A22mx8eP6ffDr7hCMqBAwoWRs7JUKrEOz%2BGIjeqseRp2UfZFK6JrrLrMK6comUVvN0zREkKh1M) | | Paid for by Blumenthal for Connecticut

Peter,

Our race has changed quite a bit recently, but my priorities for 2024 haven’t changed at all. With Joe Manchin now out, I’m laser-focused on:

1. **Stopping Chuck Schumer from interfering in West Virginia.**
2. **Helping Republicans across the Country win in crucial battleground states.**

[**These priorities may seem simple and obvious, but they will take A LOT of work and even more funding to make happen.**](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=452fc69eacde4331ac35b87b08c0e105&_e=B64H92swnpAkH7ULpHP8lSn0QpM43CiloVg0L_LgNms0mjUsxwZwnuDFQRiWKJ0tHPNMtDStKHSw5NJPfhcUy4k1GF2VZw_EF-iFX5Ct3EZhd-2TW9LmesCzKzkxImW-lVaoaz3DCSKKarn_g2bb-oL8vCW5ezHfq8i4dPEm0ghqqQnxPyfda7Q5LvG2qf70mJhaMbYe4jciUHCociGhN51rj3A-3pYoyEpzqkZZ5AlOIBLIiUwm5jQYCd7OBKB9NEQv30STTPYQNDHbptewuMkJsiT7l_A1BkVhul17ClqA1H3mTREZmxOVNFMtARyX-tlNdVJ18jzyYWBppawMDkJakPs2qjbyhE7f2dYWp_21ZQiEwxuw1IkWCIaXQukjrrefC2Fe7J9-BKE-5Bd8a99QTQyRnujBrNJHcayiTQBx8cc-3gZespYdzJE5V2QB)

| [**SEND MY DONATION**](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=452fc69eacde4331ac35b87b08c0e105&_e=B64H92swnpAkH7ULpHP8lSn0QpM43CiloVg0L_LgNms0mjUsxwZwnuDFQRiWKJ0tHPNMtDStKHSw5NJPfhcUy4k1GF2VZw_EF-iFX5Ct3EZhd-2TW9LmesCzKzkxImW-lVaoaz3DCSKKarn_g2bb-oL8vCW5ezHfq8i4dPEm0ghqqQnxPyfda7Q5LvG2qf70mJhaMbYe4jciUHCociGhN51rj3A-3pYoyEpzqkZZ5AlOIBLIiUwm5jQYCd7OBKB9NEQv30STTPYQNDHbptewuOsBSqwizctokpMIZj3H605ZONtAx7dDFTczYGyTcOXFGWs_g3-K-FNLTeDcyuMXcLZojlnuwXfvsRUKTm6YLexXnomP7fRdABkrzLxygbFG2dwAHbqhN3VarBycktyAgJk7JQNcmfuo016gx7vUTsCHi6hRde8l249RIA1HasAo)

To reinforce what we already know: _Chuck Schumer and the ultra-rich Washington Liberals have raised tens of millions of dollars to flood battleground states and put Republicans on the defense everywhere._

**But our mission is clear:** Go on the offensive and flip critical battleground states and ensure Schumer’s Washington buddies don’t get anywhere NEAR West Virginia.

[**Can I count on you today to rush a donation of $10, $15, or $20 so I can make this happen?**](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=452fc69eacde4331ac35b87b08c0e105&_e=B64H92swnpAkH7ULpHP8lSn0QpM43CiloVg0L_LgNms0mjUsxwZwnuDFQRiWKJ0tHPNMtDStKHSw5NJPfhcUy4k1GF2VZw_EF-iFX5Ct3EZhd-2TW9LmesCzKzkxImW-lVaoaz3DCSKKarn_g2bb-oL8vCW5ezHfq8i4dPEm0ghqqQnxPyfda7Q5LvG2qf70mJhaMbYe4jciUHCociGhN51rj3A-3pYoyEpzqkZZ5AlOIBLIiUwm5jQYCd7OBKB9NEQv30STTPYQNDHbptewuE1lrAQ9LWrI6ncq7tLBYF2OBW_KD0JVS1ddQuXaBmAPq1d7I7MduqG_WJYtlBM_qBz6zsqxKY0Ev7jHwMr15FOGQTiagMbPcXV3xRSNXXK_0UCXeDwzvWrjoB-7-958AYzbvlL-vQxt_FNvMBz_1cTqBkY0GDu582-RKRowF7tu)

[**SEND MY DONATION**](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=452fc69eacde4331ac35b87b08c0e105&_e=B64H92swnpAkH7ULpHP8lSn0QpM43CiloVg0L_LgNms0mjUsxwZwnuDFQRiWKJ0tHPNMtDStKHSw5NJPfhcUy4k1GF2VZw_EF-iFX5Ct3EZhd-2TW9LmesCzKzkxImW-lVaoaz3DCSKKarn_g2bb-oL8vCW5ezHfq8i4dPEm0ghqqQnxPyfda7Q5LvG2qf70mJhaMbYe4jciUHCociGhN51rj3A-3pYoyEpzqkZZ5AlOIBLIiUwm5jQYCd7OBKB9NEQv30STTPYQNDHbptewuEpYvtcilsO7vgHQ0THsbg9YTz9aJDp2uKipSJWrDuPaKuiGTQSbeeWpCH1KDVL5PQWz-JsnDiiVTKopxMwuWrBOu-myPMjxDjB5JVab2icbbqgQjglU17C_6TU7gERUOhJtnB2Z66WFkAO0U3-bkSHd_X34HVDwW74UKRqy2Wf2)

| **MEET JIM**

Gov. Jim Justice is a conservative leader, successful businessman, high school basketball coach, and devoted family man. Before being elected, Gov. Justice led over 100 companies, creating thousands of jobs.

Gov. Justice has delivered on his promise of diversifying West Virginia's economy. He enacted the largest personal income tax cut in state history. He protects traditional values and has been recognized as a pro-life champion, signing more pro-life legislation into law than any governor in state history. As an avid outdoorsman, he vigorously defends the Second Amendment. He is passionate about mentoring young people and serves as the head coach of his local high school basketball team.

Gov. Justice and his wife, First Lady Cathy Justice, have been best friends since high school. They recently celebrated their 46th wedding anniversary. The Governor and the First Lady have two adult children and spend time spoiling four grandchildren. In addition, they have three dogs: Lucy and Ellie, Boston Terriers, and their famous Babydog, an English Bulldog.
